title: Javier Bravo Bernal
text:
About

01 — About




I’m a Mechatronics Engineer with a career built across industrial automation, quality control, and service engineering in both Ecuador and New York City. My technical background includes PLC programming, electromechanical systems, and sensor integration — fields where data is generated constantly but rarely analyzed to its full potential.
That gap drove me toward data. I spent a year running the analytics side of a family healthcare business, building dashboards in Python, SQL, and Tableau that improved profitability by 8% and monthly revenue by 10% through demand forecasting and customer behavior analysis.
Today I work as a Service Engineer at Ecolab in New York, designing digital maintenance tracking systems and optimizing operations for enterprise clients. In parallel, I’m completing my M.S. in Business Analytics at Baruch College, deepening my skills in machine learning, statistical modeling, and AI-driven decision making.
I’m actively seeking roles in Business Analytics, Data Science, and AI where I can apply both my engineering precision and analytical toolkit to real business problems.



Education

M.S. Business Analytics Baruch College · 2025–Present
B.S. Mechatronics Engineering Tecnológico de Monterrey · 2017–2021

title: Javier Bravo Bernal
text:
Projects

03 — Selected Work





Project · 01 · Tableau · Marketing Analytics #### Numismática México — Holiday Sales Strategy Analyzed four years of sales data for a Mexican numismatics retailer using Tableau. Identified the star product, quantified ad lift, mapped state-level preferences, and produced a focused Q4 marketing playbook.

Tableau Marketing Analytics Data Storytelling Business Strategy

Read Analysis →




Project · 02 · Python · Machine Learning #### Credit Risk Classification — German Credit Dataset End-to-end scikit-learn ML pipeline benchmarking Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and SVM on 1,000 loan applications. Explores the recall trade-off when the cost of a missed bad loan is asymmetric.

Python scikit-learn Classification Cross-Validation

Read Analysis →




Project · 03 · R · Policy Analytics #### Campus Diversity After SFFA — National Enrollment Study Measured the demographic impact of the Supreme Court’s affirmative action ruling using 15 years of IPEDS data across 3,000+ U.S. institutions. Separates policy effects from long-term demographic trends.

R IPEDS Policy Analysis tidyverse

Read Analysis →




Project · 04 · R · Behavioral Analytics #### How Americans Spend Their Time — 20 Years of ATUS Data Mined two decades of BLS time-use microdata to analyze how full-time workers balance work, sleep, and leisure. Reveals persistent gender gaps and the delta between ideal and actual daily time allocation.

R ATUS ggplot2 Labor Economics

Read Analysis →




Project · 05 · R · Demographic Forecasting #### US Internal Migration & Congressional Reapportionment Built a migration-based demographic model using ACS flow data to forecast 2030 state populations and predict which states gain or lose seats in the 2032 congressional reapportionment via Huntington-Hill.

R tidycensus Forecasting ACS Data

Read Analysis →
